title: chenopodium × basileense
intro: Chenopodium × basileense is a hybrid species of the Chenopodium genus, resulting from the cross between Chenopodium ambrosioides and Chenopodium album, commonly known as the Mexican tea or poor man's hops. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and relieve stress. The health benefits of Chenopodium × basileense include its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various ailments. Chenopodium × basileense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
